entries.definition: Holistic, relational, directional whole. Edges matter most. Flow, emergence, behaviour.
Organisational arrangement: levels, order, layout, relative positions. Can be subset of a system; systems can emerge from it.
External, observable, material expression. Shape, medium, bounds, reach.
Lateral category or taxonomy. Things belonging together at the same level.
Vertical level within a hierarchy. Position relative to above and below.
Granularity or cardinality of composition. Size and number of components relative to the whole.
The thing-in-itself. Undivided, prior to relation, its own reference point.

A substrate of positions, each with measurable properties, related by adjacency or distance. Neutral — no position inherently marked. Parallel to /system/ (edge-oriented) and /group/ (lateral category) but specifically continuous/discrete spatial distribution.
Asymmetric contrast within a substrate, where one region is distinguished from the ground by a local difference. Structural property of the substrate, independent of observer.
Transformation extended through time. Steps visible and ordered.
Input → transformation → output. Steps hidden; only mapping matters.
Transformation where the before/after contrast is the point.
Transforming while preserving structure. Isomorphism performed, not merely observed.
A system settling into equilibrium.
Growing in magnitude, intensity, or quantity.
Shrinking in magnitude, intensity, or quantity.
Fully traversing or completing. Covering every element, leaving nothing out.
A process defined in terms of smaller instances of itself, converging toward a base.
A process generating output from an initial state, potentially unbounded.
Estimating a value within a known range, between existing data points.
Projecting a value beyond a known range, outside existing data.
Following a path backward from effect toward origin. Retrospective reconstruction.
The threshold moment of coming into existence or starting.
The threshold moment of going out of existence or stopping.
A structure that constrains and directs movement along a defined route.
Observation that two things share the same underlying structure. Isomorphism seen, not performed.
Two or more things orthogonal and stackable — compatible without interference.
Asymmetric, directional relationship. One thing requires or is downstream of another.
x produces y, directionality clear. Explicit causal arrow.
Equality, identity, equivalence of value or state.
A relationship exists between two things, type unspecified. Unmarked relational primitive.
A constraint or rule defined by prior design or external authority.
Naturally occurring regularity. Nobody made it; it repeats.
Constructed simplification or imitation made to represent something else.
Catch-all for a concept whose shape is unclear or not yet distilled.
entries.notes: Placeholder primitive — use when no other primitive fits

entries.definition: Semantic content or significance. What something points to or implies.
The chosen anchor from which all relationships are measured. Necessarily chosen, not inherent.
The zero-point within a reference frame. Where measurement or existence begins.
The boundary of what is visible or applicable from within a reference frame.
The felt quality of goodness or badness of a state. The hedonic dimension.
entries.notes: One of two axes of the circumplex model of affect. Not a judgment — a raw felt quality.

entries.definition: The felt quality of activation or energy level of a state. The energetic dimension.
entries.notes: One of two axes of the circumplex model of affect.

entries.definition: The directed orientation of a reference frame toward a target. Making something the current origin of reference.
entries.notes: Applies equally to cognitive attention, ML attention mechanisms, and social attention.

entries.definition: The axis along which events are ordered and intervals are measured.
Higher in position, rank, abstraction, or power.
Lower in position, rank, abstraction, or power.
Same level, sideways relationship. Peer, sibling.
Movement or orientation in a direction.
Movement or orientation away from something.
Fully contained within. State of containment.
Beyond the boundary of. State of exclusion.
Active containment relation. x contains y.
On the boundary itself. Neither inside nor outside.
Surrounding without containing.
The middle, the core, the focal point.
The boundary, the margin, the limit of a thing.
The mediating space between two things.
Adjacent, no containment or contact implied.
Partial shared occupancy.
Same direction, no intersection.
Orthogonal intersection at a right angle.
Touches at exactly one point without crossing.
A degree of freedom or axis of variation.
How many steps removed from the origin, and in what direction.
Precedes on a sequence, spatial or temporal.
Follows on a sequence, spatial or temporal.
Current configuration. No causal ordering implied.
Causally downstream state. Necessarily ordered relative to a cause.
A bounded span between two defined points on a sequence. The span itself is the object of interest.
Movement in a direction.
Persistence of current state or motion. Resistance to change.
Movement around an axis.
Actually obtains. Genuine, not an artefact.
Apparent but not actual. Epiphenomenal, artefact of something else.
Produced by deliberate agency.
Inherent, built-in, not separable from the thing itself.
Added from outside. Separable.
Arising from below. Self-organised, not designed.
Defined from above. Hard-coded, stipulated by external authority.
No gap. Directly adjacent, without intermediary.
Near but not immediate.
At a distance. Remote, indirect.
High intensity or magnitude.
Low intensity or magnitude.
Complete, exhaustive, leaving nothing out.
Incomplete. Some but not all.
entries.notes: Second-class: usable inline or as superscript modifier

entries.definition: Idempotent. Applying more than once has no additional effect.
entries.notes: Second-class: usable inline or as superscript modifier

entries.definition: Iterative. Accumulates change on each application.
entries.notes: Second-class: usable inline or as superscript modifier

entries.definition: Direction toward higher rank, layer, abstraction, or power.
entries.notes: Second-class: usable inline or as superscript modifier

entries.definition: Direction toward lower rank, layer, or concreteness.
entries.notes: Second-class: usable inline or as superscript modifier

entries.definition: Direction toward inside or centre.
entries.notes: Second-class: usable inline or as superscript modifier

entries.definition: Direction toward outside or edge.
entries.notes: Second-class: usable inline or as superscript modifier

entries.definition: What follows is meant explicitly, directly, outright.
What follows is figurative or approximate.
The list continues on the same principle.
Verbal / eventive. Converts head to an action or process.
entries.notes: Grammatical modifier — no standalone meaning. Written as superscript.

entries.definition: Stative / adjectival. Converts head to a property or description.
Nominal / agentive. Converts head to a thing or agent.
Inchoative / causative. To become X, or to make into X.
Reification. Treats a dynamic or operational concept as a first-class object of thought.
Genitive / sub-property. x belongs to, derives from, or is an attribute of y.
Negation. Inverts whatever it stacks on.
Exclusion. Actively does not include or permit.
Reciprocal / bidirectional. Symmetrical; every member both gives and receives.
Reflexive. Acting on or referring back to itself.
Experiential aspect. At some point, at least once, at unspecified time.
Evidentiality — direct. I observed this myself.
Evidentiality — inferential. I concluded this from evidence.
Evidentiality — hearsay. I was told this, or I read this.
Evidentiality — memory. This is from memory, possibly unreliable.
Mirativity. This contradicts my model or expectation.
entries.notes: Mirative modifier — marks moments where evidence contradicts the model

entries.definition: Neutral continuation.
Additive continuation.
Contrastive continuation.
Hypotactic consequence. What follows results from what preceded.
Comparison. Evaluate these two things relative to each other.
Analytical / epistemic stance. What follows is reasoned.
Intuitive / preference stance. What follows is felt or preferred.
Approximation. What follows is a bucket, analogy, or rough grouping.
Conceptual relationship, type unspecified. A connection exists.
What follows is a specific instance of the preceding shape.
Conditional framing.
Temporal framing. Simultaneous with.
Causal framing. What follows is because of what preceded.
Switch reference. The subject of what follows is the same entity as before.
Switch reference. The subject of what follows is a different entity.
Temporal anchor — present.
Temporal anchor — prior to now.
Temporal anchor — after now.
First person. The speaker, the origin of the utterance.
entries.notes: Gender, number, possession via superscript modifiers

entries.definition: Second person. The addressee.
Third person. Neither speaker nor addressee. Gender-neutral by default.
entries.notes: Gender added via /male/, /female/, /other/ as superscript. Number via /group/ superscript.

entries.definition: Deliberate movement to a higher layer. Elevation, upcasting, generalisation.
Deliberate movement to a lower layer. Downcasting, specialisation, concretisation.
Bidirectional causation. x causes y and y causes x.
Same object, same reference. Identity rather than equivalence.
Same value, same configuration. Structural equality.
Deliberately empty. Designed absence.
Unset, undesigned absence. Not null — never assigned.
A state outside the system's designed range. An unexpected state.
A system taking itself as its own reference frame.
Self-starting, self-organising. Causes its own beginning.
The containing structure of a causal history, traced backward to origin.
A model used as a reference frame for interpretation.
A function with no side effects. Output depends only on input.
An effect produced outside the function's scope.
Internal state hidden within its own scope.
A convention that solidified from use rather than design.
A self-containing recursive structure. A system that contains itself.
A deliberately false representation of a state.
A false perception without deliberate agency. Honest false state.
entries.notes: Applies to both clinical hallucination and LLM confabulation

entries.definition: Self-starting creation. Something that begins itself.
Self-termination. Programmed or deliberate self-destruction.
A complete bounded existence from creation to destruction.
The complement of /figure/ within a /field/ or /system/. The undifferentiated substrate against which /figure/ stands out.
A situated viewpoint. A reference frame with an origin and a scope.
entries.notes: Promoted to vocab due to frequency

entries.definition: A dimension whose value can change.
A dimension set from outside, governing behaviour.
A function mapping a state onto a quantitative dimension.
A depression that is intrinsically below and surrounded.
The visual sense.
The auditory sense.
The tactile sense.
The gustatory sense.
The olfactory sense.
The sense of body position and movement in space.
The sense of internal body state.
Completed action — marks that an action is finished; attaches immediately after the verb/verb phrase. Equivalent to Chinese 了 (le).
Ongoing action — marks that an action is in progress at the reference time. Equivalent to Chinese 在/正在 (zài/zhèngzài).
Resulting state — marks that a completed action has left a persistent state. Equivalent to Chinese 着 (zhe).
Volitional future — marks intended or planned action. Equivalent to Chinese 要 (yào). Attaches after the verb, before unaffected objects.
Epistemic possibility — marks that an action or state may occur. Equivalent to Chinese 会 (huì).
Interrogative: which person/agent. Placed in the position of the unknown constituent. "Comes from who?" = [/who/] (from) [/cause (-of)/]
Interrogative: which thing/concept. Placed in the position of the unknown constituent.
Interrogative: which location. Placed in the position of the unknown constituent. "Comes from where?" = [/where (-of)/] [/come (-do)/]
Interrogative: which time. Placed in the position of the unknown time constituent. Distinct from the marker (when) which introduces temporal clauses.
Interrogative: by what means or manner.
Interrogative: for what reason/cause. Compositionally equivalent to [/cause (-thing)/] but lexicalised for convenience.
Interrogative: which one from a set. Used when selecting from known candidates.
Interrogative: to what degree or quantity.
Any ordered grouping or list of elements
To have a particular order or sequence of items; to have an arrangement or index
